title: Alberta invokes notwithstanding clause to stop court challenges of transgender laws
author: Morgan Black
notes: Material update for EVT-033. Alberta government introduced Bill 9 (Protecting Alberta's Children Statutes Amendment Act) on November 18 2025, invoking the notwithstanding clause to shield the three transgender laws (Education Amendment Act 2024, Health Statutes Amendment Act, Fairness and Safety in Sport Act) from Charter challenge. Bill 9 passed final reading December 10 2025. This reverses Smith's December 2024 statement that she would not invoke the clause. NDP leader Naheed Nenshi said the government was 'drunk on power.' Parents and advocates said the clause would 'strip away a lifeline.' The Alberta Medical Association stated puberty blockers do not render a person infertile. Author: Morgan Black, Global News.
